title: Analytical Pipeline for Assembly and Annotation of Betta splendens Genome
author: Xin Liu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qq9dvz6

steps.instruction: Get raw sequencing data in Fastq format. Filter the raw sequencing data by using SOAPfilter (version 2.2). Using parameters '-i insertsize -y -z -p -M 2'
Estimate the genome size with k-mer (version 1.0) analysis. Using parameters 'k=17 -t 12'

1. Run SOAPdenovo (version 2.04 ) to assemble the Betta splendens genome. 2. Perform Gapcloser (version 1.12) to further close gaps in our genome obtained in step3. 1-Note: using parameters'pregraph(-K 29 -p 20);contig(-M 2);map(-k 41);scaff(<default>)' 2-Note: using reads from all insert-size libraries

1. Run RepeatModeler(1.0.8) and LTR_FINDER(1.0.6), respectively, to build de novo library based on the input assembled genome sequence. 2. Basing on the library constructed in step 5 as database, run RepeatMasker (version 3.3.0) to find and then classify the repetitive sequences. 1-Note: <default> 2-Note: using parameters '-nolow -no_is -norna -parallel 1'

Run RepeatMasker and ProteinMask (version 3.3.0) to identify repeats in the genome at DNA and protein level, respectively, by aligning sequences against existing databases, Repbase TE library (Version 17.01) and TE protein database. Using parameters 'RepeatMasker(-nolow -no_is -norna -parallel 1)ProteinMask(-noLowSimple -pvalue 0.0001)'

Run Augustus (version 3.0.3) and GlimmerHMM (version 3.0.1) to de novo predict genes in the repeat-masked genome sequences. Using parameters 'Augustus(--species=zebrafish --uniqueGeneId=true --noInFrameStop=true --gff3=on --strand=both)GlimmerHMM(-d zebrafish -f -g)' Using parameters '-d zebrafish -f -g'

Download protein sequences of homlog species (danio rerio(release-64), gadus morhua(release-65), gasterosteus aculeatus(release-64), oryzias latipes(release-64), takifugu rubripes(release-64), and tetraodon nigroviridis(release-64)), then align these against our masked genome sequences with BLAT, and then based on the BLAT mapping results, run GeneWise (version 2.2.0 ) to predict genes. Using parameters '--min_align_coverage 0.3 --max divergence rate 0.3 --extend length for both sides of regions 2000'
Integrate genes predicted in step 8-9 to obtain the consensus gene set by using GLEAN. Filtering with criterion 'overlap cutoff 0.8 and at least one homolog support'

Perform TopHat (version 2.1.0) with default parameters to align filtered RNA-seq reads against gene set mentioned in Step10, and then use Cufflinks (version 2.2.1) to assemble these transcripts, then use training parameters to predict ORFs, and finally obtain the more intergrity and trusty gene set. Filtering RNA sequencing data by SOAPnuke with parameters '-l 10 -q 0.5 -n 0.01 -Q 2”
Run BUSCO(version 3.0.1) and map final gene set and genome to actinopterygii reference to assess the completeness. Using parameters '-e 0.001 -limit 3'
